chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – Regarding the drinks, as well

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/293)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/293)
text: كَتَبْتُ إِلَيْهِ يَعْنِي أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلَ ( عليه السلام ) أَسْأَلُهُ عَنِ السِّكَنْجَبِينِ وَ الْجُلَّابِ وَ رُبِّ التُّوتِ وَ رُبِّ التُّفَّاحِ وَ رُبِّ السَّفَرْجَلِ وَ رُبِّ الرُّمَّانِ فَكَتَبَ حَلَالٌ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, form Mansour Bin Al Abbas, from Ja’far Bin Ahad Al Makfouf who said,

‘I wrote to him<sup>asws</sup>, meaning Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> the 1st asking him<sup>asws</sup> about the ginger drink, and the rose water, and the mulberry jam, and the apple jam, and the quince jam, and the pomegranate jam. So he<sup>asws</sup> wrote: ‘Permissible’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Mansur ibn al-‘Abbas from Ja‘far ibn Ahmad al-Makfuf who has said the following: “I once wrote to him, meaning, abu al-Hassan, al-Awwal, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, and asked about oxymel, rose water, jam of mulberry, apple, quince and pomegranate. He (the Imam) said in writing, ’It is lawful. 5 ”
